audit_status.source_base: Hebrew Leviticus / Vayiqra bodily discharge text tradition, draft aligned later
audit_status.review_note: First-pass draft reviewed for male discharge, semen emission, menstruation, abnormal female discharge, washing/waiting, offerings, and sanctuary protection. Strong anti-body-shame and anti-sex-shame safeguards required.

verses.restored: YHWH spoke to Moses and to Aaron, saying,
Speak to children of Israel and say to them: Any man, when discharge flows from his flesh, his discharge is unclean.
This shall be his uncleanness in his discharge: whether his flesh runs with discharge or his flesh is stopped from discharge, it is his uncleanness.
Every bed on which one with discharge lies shall be unclean, and every vessel on which he sits shall be unclean.
A man who touches his bed shall wash garments, wash in water, and be unclean until evening.
One who sits on vessel on which one with discharge sat shall wash garments, wash in water, and be unclean until evening.
One who touches flesh of one with discharge shall wash garments, wash in water, and be unclean until evening.
If one with discharge spits on clean one, then he shall wash garments, wash in water, and be unclean until evening.
Every saddle on which one with discharge rides shall be unclean.
Everyone touching anything that was under him shall be unclean until evening; one carrying them shall wash garments, wash in water, and be unclean until evening.
Everyone whom one with discharge touches without rinsing hands in water shall wash garments, wash in water, and be unclean until evening.
Earthen vessel touched by one with discharge shall be broken, and every wooden vessel shall be rinsed in water.
When one with discharge becomes clean from his discharge, he shall count seven days for his cleansing, wash garments, wash flesh in living water, and be clean.
On eighth day he shall take for himself two turtledoves or two sons of pigeon, come before YHWH to entrance of tent of meeting, and give them to priest.
Priest shall offer them, one as sin/purification offering and one as burnt offering; priest shall make atonement/covering for him before YHWH from his discharge.
When emission of seed goes out from a man, he shall wash all his flesh in water and be unclean until evening.
Every garment and every skin on which emission of seed is shall be washed in water and be unclean until evening.
A woman with whom a man lies with emission of seed, both shall wash in water and be unclean until evening.
When a woman has discharge, blood being her discharge in her flesh, seven days she shall be in her menstruation/separation; everyone touching her shall be unclean until evening.
Everything on which she lies in her menstruation/separation shall be unclean, and everything on which she sits shall be unclean.
Everyone touching her bed shall wash garments, wash in water, and be unclean until evening.
Everyone touching any vessel on which she sits shall wash garments, wash in water, and be unclean until evening.
If it is on bed or on vessel on which she sits, when he touches it, he shall be unclean until evening.
If lying, a man lies with her, and her menstruation/separation is upon him, he shall be unclean seven days; every bed on which he lies shall be unclean.

When a woman has discharge of her blood many days not in time of her menstruation/separation, or when she has flow beyond her menstruation/separation, all days of flow of her uncleanness shall be as days of her menstruation/separation; she is unclean.

Every bed on which she lies all days of her discharge shall be to her like bed of her menstruation/separation; every vessel on which she sits shall be unclean, like uncleanness of her menstruation/separation.
Everyone touching them shall be unclean, wash garments, wash in water, and be unclean until evening.
If she is clean from her discharge, she shall count for herself seven days, and afterward be clean.
On eighth day she shall take for herself two turtledoves or two sons of pigeon and bring them to priest, to entrance of tent of meeting.
Priest shall offer one as sin/purification offering and one as burnt offering; priest shall make atonement/covering for her before YHWH from discharge of her uncleanness.
You shall separate children of Israel from their uncleanness, so they do not die in their uncleanness by making unclean my dwelling-place that is in their midst.
This is Torah/instruction for one with discharge, for one from whom emission of seed goes out, becoming unclean by it,
and for one sick/menstruating in her menstruation/separation, and for one with flow of discharge, male or female, and for man who lies with unclean woman.

translation_notes: Bodily discharge impurity is ritual-state language, not moral shame or disgust.
Semen, menstruation, and genital discharges are treated as contact states affecting sanctuary approach, not as sin in themselves.
Both man and woman wash after intercourse with emission; do not single out women for shame.
Abnormal prolonged discharge receives offering after cleansing; this should not be used to shame illness.
Living water in v.13 suggests full embodied washing/restoration.
safety_note: Do not use Leviticus 15 for menstrual shame, sex shame, infertility shame, illness stigma, or disgust toward bodies. The chapter protects sanctuary boundaries through washing, waiting, and offerings; it does not declare bodies evil.
